source: Per-row citations below. External figures fetched 2026-06-11 from the KWSP i-Invest page (https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/member/savings/i-invest — kwsp.gov.my returns HTTP 403 to non-browser fetches; loaded via headless browser; sales-charge figures sit in the page's FAQ accordion). Public Mutual channel figures from owned entries corpus/knowledge/a-fee-schedule.md and corpus/knowledge/a-sales-charge-math.md (Public Mutual Master Prospectus dated 28 Aug 2023 with 1st Supplemental dated 3 Dec 2025, pp. 150-160; PEF/PAIF/PWEF/PFES PHS) — owned, not refetched. Age-banded Basic Savings schedule referenced from owned corpus/data/datasets/kwsp-ria-benchmarks.json (KWSP Table 1, verified 2026-06-07).
notes: Maximum published sales charge for buying the same family of Public Mutual equity-class unit trust funds through four different channels. The channels are not interchangeable: the agent/branch channel includes a licensed consultant's service (suitability conversation, paperwork, ongoing review); PMO is self-serve with a RM100 e-Series entry minimum; EPF i-Invest is self-serve, restricted to EPF members below 55, capped at 30% of Akaun Persaraan savings ABOVE the member's age-band Basic Savings (minimum RM1,000 per investment, eligibility recalculated every 3 months), and money invested stops earning the EPF annual dividend while it is in the funds. So the chart reads 'the charge buys different things', not 'agents overcharge'. Basis caveat: Public Mutual figures are 'of NAV per unit' (prospectus basis; 5.0% of NAV = 5/105 = 4.7619% of the gross amount paid); KWSP states i-Invest figures as 'per buy transaction'. EPF figures are explicitly 'subject to change by EPF'; Public Mutual rates 'may vary by distributor and campaign period' (owned entry). All volatility 'variable'; figures fetched/verified 2026-06-11 — re-verify before reuse after 2027-06-11.

data.channel: Agent / branch (OTC), Public Series equity funds
data.basis: of NAV per unit; = 4.7619% of gross outlay via the prospectus 5/105 formula
data.included_in_channel: Licensed consultant service: suitability conversation, paperwork, fund selection support, ongoing review. Entry minimum RM1,000 (Public Series).
data.source: Owned: corpus/knowledge/a-fee-schedule.md ('Up to 5.0% of NAV per unit' for equity/mixed-asset/balanced funds — Master Prospectus p. 150, confirmed in PEF, PAIF, PWEF, PFES PHS) and corpus/knowledge/a-sales-charge-math.md (p. 157 formula: RM10,000 x 5/105 = RM476.19 charge; 'Sales charge rates may also vary by distributor and campaign period')

data.channel: Public Mutual Online (PMO), self-serve incl. e-Series
data.basis: of NAV per unit — the prospectus cap is set per fund class, not per channel; the owned prospectus extracts publish no separate online rate
data.included_in_channel: Self-serve. e-Series funds drop the entry minimum to RM100 (vs RM1,000 Public Series) but are PMO-only and the fund range differs from the standard lineup.
data.source: Owned: corpus/knowledge/a-fee-schedule.md — the 'Up to 5.0% of NAV per unit' cap (Master Prospectus p. 150) is stated per fund class with no channel-specific online discount in the owned extracts; e-Series RM100 minimum from Public e-Series Master Prospectus p. 60. Actual charged rates may be below the cap by distributor and campaign period (owned corpus/knowledge/a-sales-charge-math.md)

data.channel: EPF-MIS via FMI agent / counter (Akaun Persaraan money)
data.basis: KWSP: 'per buy transaction'; Public Mutual prospectus: 'of NAV per unit, as regulated by EPF' — both cap the same channel at 3%
data.included_in_channel: Agent assistance with Form KWSP 9N (AHL); subject to the same i-Invest eligibility formula and caps (row i-invest-eligibility).
data.source: KWSP i-Invest page FAQ, verbatim: 'Initial sales charge by FMI is up to 3% per buy transaction (subject to change by EPF).' — column 'Application through FMI's Agent/Counter' (https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/member/savings/i-invest, fetched 2026-06-11). Corroborated by owned corpus/knowledge/a-fee-schedule.md: 'Investments under the EPF MIS will be levied a sales charge of up to 3% of NAV per unit, as regulated by EPF' (Master Prospectus p. 150)

data.channel: EPF i-Invest via KWSP i-Akaun app / web portal (self-serve)
data.included_in_channel: Fully self-serve: member registers on i-Akaun, picks funds and FMIs alone. Same eligibility formula and caps as EPF-MIS (row i-invest-eligibility); invested amount stops earning the EPF annual dividend while invested.
data.source: KWSP i-Invest page FAQ, verbatim: 'Initial sales charge by FMI is up to 0.5% per buy transaction (subject to change by EPF).' — column 'Application through KWSP i-Akaun app or i-Akaun (Member) web portal' (https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/member/savings/i-invest, fetched 2026-06-11)

data.channel: EPF i-Invest / EPF-MIS — eligibility and caps (constraint row, not charted)

eligibility.who: Malaysians, Permanent Residents, or non-Malaysians registered as EPF members before 1 Aug 1998; below 55 years of age; sufficient savings in Akaun Persaraan
eligibility.eligibility_refresh: investment eligibility amount updated every 3 months
eligibility.top_ups: members are not allowed to make additional investments using their own funds
eligibility.dividend: amount invested under the scheme is not entitled to EPF's annual dividend
eligibility.basic_savings_anchor: Basic Savings is age-banded, targets RM390,000 at age 60; latest revision effective 1 January 2026, phased in over 5 years at +RM30,000/yr (see owned corpus/data/datasets/kwsp-ria-benchmarks.json for the full age schedule)
eligibility.age_55_plus: members 55+ use the separate Age 55/60 Withdrawal (Investment) — not part of MIS; Akaun 55/Akaun Emas: any amount retaining RM1,000 in the account, Basic Savings requirement not applicable, minimum RM1,000, i-Akaun only

data.source: KWSP i-Invest page, verbatim extracts: 'Up to 30% of the total savings exceeding the Basic Savings in Akaun Persaraan. Eligibility formula: (Akaun Persaraan - Basic Savings) x 30%. Minimum investment amount is RM1,000.'; 'Investment eligibility amount will be updated every 3 months.'; 'Member are not allowed to make additional investments using their own funds.'; 'Amount invested under this scheme is not entitled to EPF's annual dividend.'; 'Basic Savings is a pre-determined amount set according to age in Akaun Persaraan to enable members to achieve a minimum savings of RM390,000 when they reach age 60. ... The latest revision to this quantum takes effect from 1 January 2026.' (https://www.kwsp.gov.my/en/member/savings/i-invest, fetched 2026-06-11)
